effect: 10,000,000 Volt Thunderbolt is an Electric type Z-Move exclusive to Cap Pikachu . It is used with Thunderbolt . 10,000,000 Volt Thunderbolt deals damage and has a sharply increased critical hit ratio ( 1 ⁄ 2 instead of 1 ⁄ 24 ).

Absorb deals damage and the user will recover 50% of the HP drained. If the user is holding a Big Root , the move instead recovers 65% of the damage dealt (30% more than normal). If used on a Pokémon with the ability Liquid Ooze , the user instead loses the HP it would have otherwise gained.

Acid deals damage and has a 10% chance of lowering the target's Special Defense by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.

Acrobatics deals damage, however, if the user is not holding an item, its power doubles to 110 . If the user is holding a Flying Gem , it is consumed before the Pokémon attacks, so it receives both boosts and increases Acrobatics' power to 143 (or 165 in Generation 5 ). Once the Gem is consumed Acrobatics' power for the rest of the battle will be 110.

Acupressure raises a random stat - Attack, Defense, Speed, Special Attack, Special Defense, Accuracy or Evasion - by two stages. It will always choose a stat that is not already maximized. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each. Acupressure is unlike other status moves in that it can be targeted at itself or an adjacent teammate. It cannot be used on an ally if they are behind a Substitute , but the user can apply Acupressure to itself if behind a Substitute.
Aerial Ace deals damage and ignores changes to the Accuracy and Evasion stats. However, it will not hit Pokémon during the invulnerable stage of Bounce , Dig , Dive , Fly , Shadow Force or Sky Drop .

Autotomize raises the user's Speed by two stages and reduces its weight by 100kg (to a minimum of 0.1kg). Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each. Decreasing weight affects these moves: Grass Knot (lower damage when receiving) Heat Crash (lower damage when attacking, higher damage when receiving) Heavy Slam (lower damage when attacking, higher damage when receiving) Low Kick (lower damage when receiving) The effect of the weight change cannot be Baton-Passed (but the Speed change is passed as normal).

Baneful Bunker prevents any attacks targeted at the user from striking, for the duration of the turn. If a contact move is targeted at the Baneful Bunker user on that turn, the attacker becomes poisoned. It has priority +4 so will activate before most other moves. Poison or Steel type Pokémon, those with the ability Immunity or those behind a Substitute cannot be poisoned. The effects do not stack with the Rocky Helmet item or the Rough Skin or Iron Barbs abilities. The accuracy of Baneful Bunker drops by 1 ⁄ 3 each time it is used consecutively after itself or Protect , Detect , Endure , Wide Guard , Quick Guard , Spiky Shield , or King's Shield .
Barb Barrage deals damage and has a 50% chance chance of poisoning the target. If the target is already poisoned, its power is doubled.

Baton Pass causes the user to switch out for another Pokémon, passing any stat changes to the Pokémon that switches in. These effects are passed: Increases and decreases in Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense and Speed. For example the effects from Nasty Plot , Swords Dance and Screech (if targeted at the user of Baton Pass ). The sure hit effect from Lock-On and Mind Reader . In other words, after using either move followed by Baton Pass , the next move from the incoming Pokémon will always hit. The confusion status ailment. A Substitute , which keeps its remaining HP. Trapping effects from Mean Look , Block and Spider Web , on both the user and opponent. Critical hit ratio increase from the move Focus Energy or a Lansat Berry. The HP-restoring effects of Aqua Ring and Ingrain . The HP-depleting effect of Curse (when used by a Ghost -type on the user). The effects of the moves Embargo , Gastro Acid , Magnet Rise , Perish Song and Power Trick .

Beat Up deals damage from each Pokémon on your team that does not have a status ailment (nor is fainted). Each strike has a different base power , calculated as: However, the actual attack is carried out by the original user of Beat Up , so it will always use that Pokémon's current attack stat to calculate damage. It will also receive any boosts from the originating user, such as STAB and any applicable items. However, any items held by the rest of the team do not affect the damage dealt. Each strike of Beat Up is treated like a separate attack: Each strike can get a critical hit independently. If the Sturdy ability, Focus Sash , or Focus Band activates before the final hit, the subsequent hit will still deal damage and therefore cause the opponent to faint. If the first hit breaks a Substitute , the second hit will deal damage to the target Pokémon. The move Counter only counters the final hit, but Bide counters the complete damage.

Bestow gives the user's held item to the target. It will not work if the target is already holding an item or is behind a Substitute . Mail cannot be transferred. Items bestowed upon wild Pokémon are permanently lost if the Pokémon is KO'd, but items lost in Trainer battles are returned. All held items in wi-fi/linked battles (including Battle Tower/Subway/Maison) are returned to their respective owners at the end of a battle. Pokemon with the ability Sticky Hold cannot have their held item removed. The Griseous Orb cannot be removed from Giratina ; Type Plates cannot be removed from Arceus ; Drives cannot be removed from Genesect ; and Mega Stones cannot be removed from their respective Pokémon.

The user of Bide stores energy for 2 turns. At the end of the second turn the Pokémon unleashes energy , dealing twice the HP damage it received. Bide deals fixed, typeless damage, so will hit Ghost -type Pokémon. It also ignores changes to the Accuracy and Evasion stats and can hit Pokémon in the invulnerable stage of Bounce , Dig , Dive , Fly , Phantom Force , Shadow Force or Sky Drop . In double and triple battles, Bide hits the last Pokémon to attack the user (including teammates).

Conversion changes the user's type to the same type as the first move in the Pokémon's move list. For example, if the Pokémon has the moves Psybeam , Discharge , Recover and Conversion in that order, Conversion will change the user's type to Psychic .

Items stolen from the player's Pokémon, either by wild Pokémon or Trainer Pokémon, are returned at the end of the battle. All held items in wi-fi/linked battles (including Battle Tower/Subway/Maison) are returned to their respective owners at the end of a battle. If the item would activate on attack (e.g. Focus Band ), it does so before being stolen. If the item is consumable on attack (e.g. Focus Sash ) then the item is consumed as normal and cannot be stolen. Pokemon with the ability Sticky Hold cannot have their held item removed. The Griseous Orb cannot be removed from Giratina ; Type Plates cannot be removed from Arceus ; Drives cannot be removed from Genesect ; and Mega Stones cannot be removed from their respective Pokémon.
Crabhammer deals damage and has an increased critical hit ratio ( 1 ⁄ 8 instead of 1 ⁄ 24 ).

Crafty Shield protects the user's team from any Status category move targeted at them, for the duration of the turn. It has priority +3 so will activate before most other moves. It does not protect against the moves Perish Song , Spikes , Stealth Rock , Toxic Spikes , or Sticky Web ; and it does not prevent moves that target the attacker itself (such as Rest ).
Cross Chop deals damage and has an increased critical hit ratio ( 1 ⁄ 8 instead of 1 ⁄ 24 ).

Cross Poison deals damage and has a 10% chance of poisoning the target. It also has an increased critical hit ratio ( 1 ⁄ 8 instead of 1 ⁄ 24 ). Poison or Steel type Pokémon, those with the ability Immunity or those behind a Substitute cannot be poisoned.
Crunch deals damage and has a 20% chance of lowering the target's Defense by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Crush Claw deals damage and has a 50% chance of lowering the target's Defense by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.

Crush Grip inflicts more damage when the opponent's HP is higher. At full HP its base power is 120 and it decreases in proportion - i.e. the power is: Crush Grip is a Physical-category counterpart move to Wring Out . It is also similar to Water Spout and Eruption , based on the opponent rather than the user.

Curse is a unique move that works differently for Ghost -type Pokémon than other Pokémon. Ghost-type Pokémon use 50% of their maximum HP to place a curse on the target. The cursed Pokémon loses 25% of their maximum HP every turn. If the user has less than 50% HP remaining, they will faint but the curse still takes effect. For all other Pokémon, Curse raises the user's Attack and Defense by one stage each and lowers Speed by one stage. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each, or lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Cut deals damage with no additional effect in battle.

Dark Pulse deals damage and has a 20% chance of causing the target to flinch (if the target has not yet moved). Pokémon with the ability Inner Focus or those behind a Substitute cannot be made to flinch.

Dark Void puts the target(s) to sleep. It can only be used by Darkrai . It targets all adjacent opponents i.e. the opposing Pokémon in a single battle, both opponents in a double battle, three opponents if in the center of a triple battle, or two opponents if situated at the side in a triple battle. Each target is counted as a separate attack so it may succeed for all, any or none of the targets. Sleeping Pokémon cannot move (with some exceptions such as Snore ). Sleep lasts for 1-3 turns. Pokémon with the abilities Insomnia or Vital Spirit , or those behind a Substitute cannot be put to sleep.
Darkest Lariat deals damage, but ignores changes to the target's stats (i.e. Defense and Evasion in this case).
Dazzling Gleam deals damage and hits all adjacent opponents in double/triple battles.
Sharply raises target's Attack and Special Attack.							(100% chance)
Defend Order raises the user's Defense and Special Defense by one stage each. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.

Defense Curl raises the user's Defense by one stage. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each. After Defense Curl has been used at least once and while the user stays in battle, the power of Rollout and Ice Ball is double their regular base power.

Defog lowers the target's Evasion by one stage and removes entry hazards and barriers on both sides of the field. The moves affected are: Light Screen , Reflect , Mist , Safeguard , Spikes , Stealth Rock , Sticky Web and Toxic Spikes . Defog also clears foggy weather in battle (which causes Accuracy loss when present), however this weather condition is only present in Sinnoh (Diamond/Pearl/Platinum) and cannot be created in battle otherwise.

If the user faints, the opponent also faints. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Ghostium Z and uses its Z-Power, Destiny Bond turns into Z-Destiny Bond and makes the Pokémon become the center of attention, in addition to its usual effect as above.

Detect prevents any attacks targeted at the user from striking, for the duration of the turn. It has priority +4 so will activate before most other moves. The move functions identically to Protect . The accuracy of Detect drops by 1 ⁄ 3 each time it is used consecutively after itself or Protect , Endure , Wide Guard , Quick Guard , Spiky Shield , King's Shield , or Baneful Bunker . Damage-dealing Z-Moves bypass Detect but deal 1 ⁄ 4 of the damage.
Devastating Drake is the Dragon type Z-Move, introduced in Pokémon Sun/Moon.
Diamond Storm deals damage and has a 50% chance of raising the user's Defense by two stages. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.

The user of Dig will burrow its way underground on the first turn, disappearing from view and becoming invulnerable to most attacks. On the second turn, Dig deals damage. While underground, the Pokémon can only be hit by the moves Earthquake , Magnitude or Fissure , with Earthquake and Magnitude dealing twice normal damage. Moves from No Guard Pokémon, or any move following an identify move can also hit for regular power. The user also avoids end-of-turn damage from Sandstorm and Hail when underground. If the user is holding a Power Herb , Dig executes in one turn.

Dire Claw deals damage and has a 50% chance of either poisoning, paralyzing, or putting the target to sleep. Poison or Steel type Pokémon, those with the ability Immunity or those behind a Substitute cannot be poisoned. Electric type Pokémon, those with the ability Limber or those behind a Substitute cannot be paralyzed. Pokémon with the abilities Insomnia or Vital Spirit , or those behind a Substitute cannot be put to sleep.
Disable causes the previous move the target used to be disabled for 1-8 turns, which prevents the move's use.

Disarming Voice deals damage and ignores changes to the Accuracy and Evasion stats. However, it will not hit Pokémon during the invulnerable stage of Bounce , Dig , Dive , Fly , Shadow Force or Sky Drop .
Discharge deals damage and has a 30% chance of paralyzing the target(s). Electric type Pokémon, those with the ability Limber or those behind a Substitute cannot be paralyzed.

The user of Dive will hide underwater on the first turn, disappearing from view and becoming invulnerable to most attacks. On the second turn, Dive deals damage. While underwater, the Pokémon can only be hit by the moves Surf , Whirlpool or Low Kick , with Surf and Whirlpool dealing twice normal damage. Moves from No Guard Pokémon, or any move following an identify move can also hit for regular power. The user also avoids end-of-turn damage from Sandstorm and Hail when underwater. If the user is holding a Power Herb , Dive executes in one turn.
Dizzy Punch deals damage and has a 20% chance of confusing the target. Pokémon with the ability Own Tempo or those behind a Substitute cannot be confused.
Changes the abilities of the user and its teammates to that of the target.

Two turns after the move is selected, Doom Desire will deal damage. If the opponent switched Pokémon in the meantime, the damage dealt will depend on the Special Defense of the Pokémon the move hits, not the Pokémon it was originally targeted at.

Double Hit deals damage and will strike twice (with 35 base power each time). Each strike of Double Hit is treated like a separate attack: Each strike can get a critical hit independently. Abilities that activate on contact ( Static , Weak Armor , Iron Barbs etc.) activate for each strike. If the Sturdy ability, Focus Sash , or Focus Band activates before the final hit, the subsequent hit will still deal damage and therefore cause the opponent to faint. If the first hit breaks a Substitute , the second hit will deal damage to the target Pokémon. The move Counter only counters the final hit, but Bide counters the complete damage.

Double Iron Bash deals damage, will strike twice (with 60 base power each time), and has a 30% chance of causing the target to flinch (if the target has not yet moved). Pokémon with the ability Inner Focus or those behind a Substitute cannot be made to flinch.

Double Kick deals damage and will strike twice (with 30 base power each time). Each strike of Double Kick is treated like a separate attack: Each strike can get a critical hit independently. Abilities that activate on contact ( Static , Weak Armor , Iron Barbs etc.) activate for each strike. If the Sturdy ability, Focus Sash , or Focus Band activates before the final hit, the subsequent hit will still deal damage and therefore cause the opponent to faint. If the first hit breaks a Substitute , the second hit will deal damage to the target Pokémon. The move Counter only counters the final hit, but Bide counters the complete damage.
After using this move, the user will no longer be Electric type.

Hits Prob. Power 2 3 ⁄ 8 30 3 3 ⁄ 8 45 4 1 ⁄ 8 60 5 1 ⁄ 8 75 Double Slap hits 2-5 times per turn used. The probability of each interval is shown in the table, with the total power after each hit. Each strike of Double Slap is treated like a separate attack: Each strike can get a critical hit independently. Abilities that activate on contact ( Static , Weak Armor , Iron Barbs etc.) activate for each strike. If the Sturdy ability, Focus Sash , or Focus Band activates before the final hit, the subsequent hit will still deal damage and therefore cause the opponent to faint. If the first hit breaks a Substitute , the second hit will deal damage to the target Pokémon. The move Counter only counters the final hit, but Bide counters the complete damage. The Skill Link ability will cause Double Slap to always hit the maximum number of times.
Double Team raises the user's Evasiveness by one stage, thus making the user more difficult to hit. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.
Double-Edge deals damage, but the user receives 1 ⁄ 3 of the damage it inflicted in recoil. In other words, if the attack does 90 HP damage to the opponent, the user will lose 30 HP.
Draco Meteor deals damage but lowers the user's Special Attack by two stages after attacking. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Dragon Ascent deals damage but lowers the user's Defense and Special Defense by one stage each after attacking. It is the signature move of Rayquaza , and once learned allows Rayquaza to Mega-Evolve.
Dragon Breath deals damage and has a 30% chance of paralyzing the target. Electric type Pokémon, those with the ability Limber or those behind a Substitute cannot be paralyzed.
The user raises its allies’ morale with a draconic cry so that their future attacks have a heightened chance of landing critical hits. This rouses Dragon types more.
Dragon Claw deals damage with no additional effect.
Dragon Dance raises the user's Attack and Speed by one stage each. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.
Dragon Darts deals damage and hits twice. In a double battle, it hits each opponent once. Dragon Darts is the signature move of Dragapult .
The higher the user's HP, the higher the power.

The user uses its body like a hammer to attack the target and inflict damage. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Dragonium Z and uses its Z-Power, Dragon Hammer turns into Devastating Drake and has base power 175 .
Dragon Pulse deals damage with no additional effect.
Dragon Rage always deals 40 HP damage to the target, regardless of typing. It has no additional effect.

Dragon Rush deals damage and has a 20% chance of causing the target to flinch (if the target has not yet moved). Pokémon with the ability Inner Focus or those behind a Substitute cannot be made to flinch. If the target has used Minimize , Dragon Rush ignores accuracy and deals double damage.

In battles, the opponent switches. In the wild, the Pokémon runs. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Dragonium Z and uses its Z-Power, Dragon Tail turns into Devastating Drake and has base power 120 .

Drain Punch deals damage and the user will recover 50% of the HP drained. If the user is holding a Big Root, the move instead recovers 65% of the damage dealt (30% more than normal). If used on a Pokémon with the ability Liquid Ooze , the user instead loses the HP it would have otherwise gained.
Draining Kiss deals damage and the user will recover 75% of the HP drained.

Dream Eater deals damage only on sleeping foes and the user will recover 50% of the HP drained. If the user is holding a Big Root , the move instead recovers 65% of the damage dealt (30% more than normal). If used on a Pokémon with the ability Liquid Ooze , the user instead loses the HP it would have otherwise gained.
Drill Peck deals damage with no additional effect, besides being able to hit non-adjacent Pokémon in triple battles.
Drill Run deals damage and has an increased critical hit ratio ( 1 ⁄ 8 instead of 1 ⁄ 24 ).
Lowers opponent's Speed.							(100% chance)

Dual Chop deals damage and will strike twice (with 40 base power each time). Each strike of Dual Chop is treated like a separate attack: Each strike can get a critical hit independently. Abilities that activate on contact ( Static , Weak Armor , Iron Barbs etc.) activate for each strike. If the Sturdy ability, Focus Sash , or Focus Band activates before the final hit, the subsequent hit will still deal damage and therefore cause the opponent to faint. If the first hit breaks a Substitute , the second hit will deal damage to the target Pokémon. The move Counter only counters the final hit, but Bide counters the complete damage.
The user slams the target with its wings. The target is hit twice in a row. Dual Wingbeat is a tutor move available in The Isle of Armor DLC.
Damage doubles if opponent is Dynamaxed.

Dynamic Punch deals damage and confuses the target, if it hits. Confused Pokémon have a 33% chance of hurting themselves each turn, for 1-4 attacking turns (50% chance in Generations 1-6 ). The damage received is as if the Pokémon attacks itself with a typeless 40 base power Physical attack. Pokémon with the ability Own Tempo or those behind a Substitute cannot be confused.
Earth Power deals damage and has a 10% chance of lowering the target's Special Defense by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Earthquake deals damage, and will hit with double power if the opponent is underground due to the move Dig .

Echoed Voice deals damage starting at base power 40, and increases by 40 each turn if used by any Pokémon on the field, up to a maximum base power of 200. It does not reset if it misses, as long as the move is attempted each turn. The power does not increase during a turn (if multiple Pokémon use it), only after each turn.
Eerie Impulse lowers the target's Special Attack by two stages. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Eerie Spell deals damage and reduces the PP of the target's last-used move by 3.
Egg Bomb deals damage with no additional effect.

Electric Terrain creates a low-lying electric field lasting 5 turns, that prevents grounded Pokémon from falling asleep. The power of Electric type moves also increases by 50% while in effect. Grounded Pokémon are simply those that are not raised . Raised Pokémon are any Flying types or those under the effects of Levitate , Magnet Rise or Telekinesis . Full details in glossary . Electric Terrain is a terrain condition and can coexist at the same time as weather conditions, but not other terrain conditions. The move Nature Power turns into Thunderbolt under Electric Terrain .
Electrify causes the target's move to become Electric type, if it has yet to attack during that turn.

Ratio (r) Power 4 ≤ r 150 3 ≤ r < 4 120 2 ≤ r < 3 80 1 ≤ r < 2 60 r < 1 40 Electro Ball inflicts more damage the faster the user is compared to the opponent. The table shows the different base powers for each ratio, where r = UserSpeed ÷ TargetSpeed For example, if the user's Speed is at least 3 times more than the target but less than 4 times, Electro Ball hits with 120 base power.
Boosted even more if it's super-effective.
The user absorbs electricity on the first turn and raises its Special Attack; on the second turn, Electro Shot deals damage. During rain , Electro Shot executes in one turn.
Electroweb deals damage and lowers the target's Speed by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Embargo prevents the target using any items for five turns. This includes both held items and items used by the trainer such as medicines.
Ember deals damage and has a 10% chance of burning the target. Fire type Pokémon, those with the ability Water Veil or those behind a Substitute cannot be burned.
Encore forces the target to use the previously selected move for 3 turns. Encore does not work against Encore itself, Mimic , Mirror Move , Sketch , Struggle or Transform .
Endeavor reduces the opponent's HP to equal the user's. (It fails if the user's HP is higher than the target's.)

Endure allows the user to survive all attacks during the turn with at least 1 HP (even if it already has 1 HP remaining). It has priority +4 so will activate before most other moves. The accuracy of Endure decreases by 1 ⁄ 3 each time it is used consecutively after itself or Protect , Detect , Wide Guard , Quick Guard , Spiky Shield , King's Shield , or Baneful Bunker .
Energy Ball deals damage and has a 10% chance of lowering the target's Special Defense by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Entrainment makes the target's ability the same as the user's. It does not work on Pokémon with the abilities Truant , Multitype or Zen Mode .

Eruption inflicts more damage when the user's HP is higher. At full HP its base power is 150 and it decreases in proportion - i.e. the power is: Eruption is a counterpart move to Water Spout . It is also similar to Crush Grip and Wring Out , based on the user rather than the opponent.
Esper Wing deals damage and raises the user's Speed by one stage. It also has an increased critical hit ratio ( 1 ⁄ 8 instead of 1 ⁄ 24 ). Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.
User can't move on the next turn.
The user attacks the target with its psychic power. This move’s power goes up and damages all opposing Pokémon on Psychic Terrain. Expanding Force is a tutor move available in The Isle of Armor DLC.
Explosion deals high damage, but causes the user to faint.

Extrasensory deals damage and has a 10% chance of causing the target to flinch (if the target has not yet moved). Pokémon with the ability Inner Focus or those behind a Substitute cannot be made to flinch.

Extreme Evoboost is a Z-Move exclusive to Eevee . It is used with Last Resort . Extreme Evoboost raises all the user's stats (Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense, Speed) by two stages each.
Extreme Speed deals damage and attacks before the majority of other moves (priority of +2).

Facade deals damage, and hits with double power (140) if the user is burned, poisoned or paralyzed. In the case of a burn, the usual attack-halving still occurs so Facade hits with an effective power of 70.
Fairy Lock prevents all Pokémon on the field from fleeing during the following turn.
Fairy Wind deals damage with no added effects.

Fake Out attacks with a priority of +3 and causes the target to flinch if it hits. It is only successful on the first turn the Pokémon is in battle, but reset each time the Pokémon switches out and back in. Pokémon with the ability Inner Focus or those behind a Substitute cannot be made to flinch.
Fake Tears lowers the target's Special Defense by two stages. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Ignores Accuracy and Evasiveness.
Always leaves opponent with at least 1 HP. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Normalium Z and uses its Z-Power, False Swipe turns into Breakneck Blitz and has base power 100 .
Feather Dance lowers the target's Attack by two stages. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.

Feint only hits if the target used Protect or Detect in the same turn. It also cancels Protect/Detect for the remainder of the turn, so subsequent moves in the same turn (in double/triple battles) can still hit.

Feint Attack deals damage and ignores changes to the Accuracy and Evasion stats. However, it will not hit Pokémon during the invulnerable stage of Bounce , Dig , Dive , Fly , Phantom Force , Shadow Force or Sky Drop .
Fell Stinger deals damage, and if the target is knocked out by this move, the user's Attack is raised by three stages. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.
Fickle Beam deals damage with 80 base power, however there is a 30% chance for the power to double to 160.
Fiery Dance deals damage and has a 50% chance of raising the user's Special Attack by one stage. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.
May cause flinching.							(20% chance)
Lowers HP but sharply boosts Attack, Special Attack, and Speed.

Inflicts damage equal to the user's remaining HP. User faints. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Fightinium Z and uses its Z-Power, Final Gambit turns into All-Out Pummeling and has base power 180 .
Fire Blast deals damage and has a 10% chance of burning the target. Fire type Pokémon, those with the ability Water Veil or those behind a Substitute cannot be burned.

Fire Fang deals damage, has a 10% chance of burning the target and has a 10% chance of causing the target to flinch (if the target has not yet moved). Fire type Pokémon, those with the ability Water Veil or those behind a Substitute cannot be burned. Pokémon with the ability Inner Focus or those behind a Substitute cannot be made to flinch.

The user strikes the target with a burning lash. This also lowers the target's Defense stat.							(100% chance) Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Firium Z and uses its Z-Power, Fire Lash turns into Inferno Overdrive and has base power 160 .

Fire Pledge deals damage at base power 80 . If a teammate uses Grass Pledge or Water Pledge in the same turn, the moves combine into one attack of base power 160 and added effects appear. The faster Pokémon moves first but waits for the other Pokémon to make their move . When the slower Pledge user moves, the combo attack hits the Pokémon the slower user was targeting. If Fire Pledge is used with Grass Pledge , the move animation shows Fire Pledge and a sea of fire envelops the foe's team is created that damages opponents 1 ⁄ 8 of their maximum HP each turn, for 4 turns. If Fire Pledge is used with Water Pledge , the move animation shows Water Pledge and a rainbow appears in the sky on your team's side that doubles the chance of moves' secondary effects occurring for 4 turns.
Fire Punch deals damage and has a 10% chance of burning the target. Fire type Pokémon, those with the ability Water Veil or those behind a Substitute cannot be burned.

Fire Spin inflicts damage on the first turn then traps the opponent, causing them to lose 1 ⁄ 8 of their maximum HP after each turn, for 4-5 turns. If the user holds a Grip Claw , the after-effects last for 7 turns. If a Binding Band is held, the damage is 1 ⁄ 6 of the target's maximum HP.

Although this move has great power, it only works the first turn the user is in battle. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Buginium Z and uses its Z-Power, First Impression turns into Savage Spin-Out and has base power 175 .
If the user attacks before the target, the power of this move is doubled.

If it hits, Fissure is guaranteed to make the opponent faint. Its accuracy increases if the user is a higher level than the target Pokémon, but fails if the target is higher level. Its accuracy (as a percentage) is calculated as below, ignoring all other accuracy and evasion modifiers: This means that if the user and target Pokémon are the same level, Fissure has 30% accuracy.

Flail deals more damage the lower the user's HP. The following formula is used: The value of N is then used to determine the base power, as per the table below. Flame Burst deals damage and will also cause splash damage to any Pokémon adjacent to the target (including the user if Flame Burst is targeted at an ally). The splash damage caused to a Pokémon is 1 ⁄ 16 of its maximum HP. It is typeless and not counted as regular damage but treated similarly to weather damage, which means that: It does not trigger Flash Fire or Color Change . It does not affect Pokémon with the Magic Guard ability. It cannot be shielded by moves like Protect , Endure or other protection moves. It bypasses Wonder Guard , Mold Breaker , Telepathy , Substitute and Destiny Bond .
Flame Charge deals damage and raises the user's Speed by one stage. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.

Flame Wheel deals damage and has a 10% chance of burning the target. Fire type Pokémon, those with the ability Water Veil or those behind a Substitute cannot be burned. If the user is frozen, Flame Wheel can be used and will thaw out the Pokémon before attacking.
Flamethrower deals damage and has a 10% chance of burning the target. Fire type Pokémon, those with the ability Water Veil or those behind a Substitute cannot be burned.

Flare Blitz deals damage and has a 10% chance of burning the target, but the user receives 1 ⁄ 3 of the damage it inflicted in recoil. In other words, if the attack does 90 HP damage to the opponent, the user will lose 30 HP. Fire type Pokémon, those with the ability Water Veil or those behind a Substitute cannot be burned. If the user is frozen, Flare Blitz can be used and will thaw out the Pokémon before attacking.

Flash lowers the target's Accuracy by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each. Pokémon with the abilities Keen Eye , Clear Body or White Smoke cannot have their accuracy reduced.
Flash Cannon deals damage and has a 10% chance of lowering the target's Special Defense by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.

Flatter confuses the target, and raises its Special Attack by one stage. If one of the two effects cannot be invoked (for example the target is already confused or its Special Attack already raised to the maximum of +6 stages), Flatter still works and will invoke the other effect. Confused Pokémon have a 33% chance of hurting themselves each turn, for 1-4 attacking turns (50% chance in Generations 1-6 ). The damage received is as if the Pokémon attacks itself with a typeless 40 base power Physical attack.
Fleur Cannon deals damage but lowers the user's Special Attack by two stages after attacking. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Power depends on held item. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Darkinium Z and uses its Z-Power, Fling turns into Black Hole Eclipse and has base power 100 .
After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting. Flip Turn is a tutor move available in The Isle of Armor DLC.

Floaty Fall deals damage and has a 30% chance of causing the target to flinch (if the target has not yet moved). Floaty Fall is a Pikachu-exclusive tutor move from Pokémon Let's Go, along with Zippy Zap and Splishy Splash .

The user restores the target's HP by up to half of its max HP. It restores more HP when the terrain is grass. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Fairium Z and uses its Z-Power, Floral Healing turns into Z-Floral Healing and eliminates any stat drops applied to the user, in addition to its usual effect as above.
Flower Shield raises the Defense of all Grass-type Pokémon in battle (including opponents) by one stage. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.
Never misses; always results in a critical hit.

The user of Fly will fly up high on the first turn, disappearing from view and becoming invulnerable to most attacks. On the second turn, Fly deals damage. While in the air, the Pokémon can only be hit by the moves Gust , Twister , Thunder , Sky Uppercut and Smack Down , with Gust and Twister dealing twice normal damage. Moves from No Guard Pokémon, or any move following an identify move can also hit for regular power. If the user is holding a Power Herb , Fly executes in one turn.

Flying Press deals damage. It is a Fighting type move, but deals a combination of Fighting and Flying type damage. This in effect gives the move its own type chart, as shown below. Nor Fir Wat Ele Gra Ice Fig Poi Gro Fly Psy Bug Roc Gho Dra Dar Ste Fai 2 ½ 2 2 2 ½ ½ ½ 0 2 ½ Although Flying Press deals dual type damage, it is still a pure Fighting type move so only gains STAB from Fighting type Pokémon. If the target has used Minimize , Flying Press ignores accuracy and deals double damage.
Focus Blast deals damage and has a 10% chance of lowering the target's Special Defense by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.

Increases critical hit ratio. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Normalium Z and uses its Z-Power, Focus Energy turns into Z-Focus Energy and raises Accuracy one stage, in addition to its usual effect as above.

The user of Focus Punch will tighten its focus before any other moves are made. If any regular move (with a higher priority than -3) directly hits the focused Pokémon, it loses its focus and flinches, not carrying out the attack. If no direct hits are made, Focus Punch attacks as normal. Moves of lower priority than Focus Punch - for example Avalanche or Dragon Tail - will not cause the user to lose focus. The user will tighten their focus, Focus Punch will attack, then the opponent will use their lower priority move (and in the case of Avalanche , hit with double power).
In Double Battle, the user takes all the attacks.
Force Palm deals damage and has a 30% chance of paralyzing the target. Electric type Pokémon, those with the ability Limber or those behind a Substitute cannot be paralyzed.

Resets opponent's Evasiveness, and allows Normal- and Fighting-type attacks to hit Ghosts. Changes In Generations 2-3 , Foresight has 100% accuracy. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Normalium Z and uses its Z-Power, Foresight turns into Z-Foresight and raises critical-hit ratio one stage, in addition to its usual effect as above.

Forest's Curse adds the Grass type to the target. If the target is dual-typed they essentially become a triple-typed Pokémon. It fails if one of the Pokémon's types is already Grass. If the target is under the effect of Trick-or-Treat the Ghost type is replaced by Grass.

Foul Play uses the opponent's Attack stat instead of the user's in damage calculation. In other words, the move acts as if the target is attacking themselves, and thus is more powerful when the opponent has higher Attack.

The user of Freeze Shock will charge on the first turn. On the second turn, it deals damage and has a 30% chance of paralyzing the target. Electric type Pokémon, those with the ability Limber or those behind a Substitute cannot be paralyzed. If the user is holding a Power Herb , Freeze Shock executes in one turn.

Freeze-Dry deals damage and has a 10% change of freezing the target. In addition to Ice's usual type advantages, Freeze-Dry is also super-effective against the Water type. This in effect gives the move its own (slightly different) type chart, as shown below: Nor Fir Wat Ele Gra Ice Fig Poi Gro Fly Psy Bug Roc Gho Dra Dar Ste Fai ½ 2 2 ½ 2 2 2 ½
May freeze opponent.							(10% chance)

Freezy Frost deals damage and eliminates stat changes from all Pokémon on the field (similar to Haze ). Freezy Frost is an Eevee-exclusive tutor move from Pokémon Let's Go, along with Bouncy Bubble , Buzzy Buzz , Sizzly Slide , Glitzy Glow , Baddy Bad , Sappy Seed , and Sparkly Swirl .
User must recharge next turn. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Grassium Z and uses its Z-Power, Frenzy Plant turns into Bloom Doom and has base power 200 .

Frost Breath deals damage and always results in a critical hit, increasing its power to 90 . Pokémon with the abilities Battle Armor or Shell Armor , or those under the effect of Lucky Chant are immune to critical hits so Frost Breath will hit them with base power 60.

The power of Frustration is higher when the Pokémon likes its trainer less. The base power varies between 1 (maximum Friendship) and 102 (minimum Friendship). The exact formula is: Pokémon Friendship (aka Happiness) is a value between 0 and 255 ( more details ). Frustration is the counterpart move to Return .

Hits Prob. Power 2 3 ⁄ 8 30 3 3 ⁄ 8 45 4 1 ⁄ 8 60 5 1 ⁄ 8 75 Fury Attack hits 2-5 times per turn used. The probability of each interval is shown in the table, with the total power after each hit. Each strike of Fury Attack is treated like a separate attack: Each strike can get a critical hit independently. Abilities that activate on contact ( Static , Weak Armor , Iron Barbs etc.) activate for each strike. If the Sturdy ability, Focus Sash , or Focus Band activates before the final hit, the subsequent hit will still deal damage and therefore cause the opponent to faint. If the first hit breaks a Substitute , the second hit will deal damage to the target Pokémon. The move Counter only counters the final hit, but Bide counters the complete damage. The Skill Link ability will cause Fury Attack to always hit the maximum number of times.

Fury Cutter deals damage starting at base power 40, and for each successful consecutive hit doubles in power, up to a maximum base power of 160. If the move misses for any reason, the base power resets to 40.

Fusion Bolt deals damage, and its power doubles to 200 if Fusion Flare has been used before it in the same turn. If this happens Fusion Bolt's animation will change to look more epic.

Fusion Flare deals damage, and its power doubles to 200 if Fusion Bolt has been used before it in the same turn. If this happens Fusion Flare's animation will change to look more epic. If the user is frozen, Fusion Flare can be used and will thaw out the Pokémon before attacking.

Future Sight deals damage, but does not hit until two turns after the move is used. If the opponent switched Pokémon in the meantime, the new Pokémon gets hit, with their type and stats taken into account. Future Sight cannot be stopped by Protect , Detect , Endure or the Wonder Guard ability.

G-Max Befuddle is a G-Max Move from Pokemon Sword and Shield , exclusive to Butterfree . Bug type moves turn into G-Max Befuddle during Gigantamax. G-Max Befuddle deals damage and causes poison, paralysis or sleep to each opponent.

G-Max Cannonade is a G-Max Move from the Pokemon Sword and Shield Expansion Pass, exclusive to Blastoise . Water type moves turn into G-Max Cannonade during Gigantamax. G-Max Cannonade deals damage and causes all non-Water type opponents to lose 1 ⁄ 6 of their maximum HP after each turn, for 4 turns.

G-Max Centiferno is a G-Max Move from Pokémon Sword and Shield , exclusive to Centiskorch . Fire type moves turn into G-Max Centiferno during Gigantamax. G-Max Centiferno deals damage and traps opponents, causing them to lose 1 ⁄ 8 of their maximum HP after each turn, for 4-5 turns, like the move Fire Spin .

G-Max Chi Strike is a G-Max Move from Pokémon Sword and Shield , exclusive to Machamp . Fighting type moves turn into G-Max Chi Strike during Gigantamax. G-Max Chi Strike deals damage and increases the user's critical hit ratio.

G-Max Cuddle is a G-Max Move from Pokemon Sword and Shield , exclusive to Eevee . Normal type moves turn into G-Max Cuddle during Gigantamax. G-Max Cuddle deals damage and infatuates all opposite-gender opponents.

G-Max Depletion is a G-Max Move from Pokémon Sword and Shield , exclusive to Duraludon . Dragon type moves turn into G-Max Depletion during Gigantamax. G-Max Depletion deals damage and reduces the PP of the last move used.

G-Max Drum Solo is a G-Max Move from the Pokemon Sword and Shield Expansion Pass, exclusive to Rillaboom . Grass type moves turn into G-Max Drum Solo during Gigantamax. G-Max Drum Solo deals damage and ignores all effects of the target's Ability. Its base power is always 160 , regardless of the original move selected, but it matches the original category (Physical or Special), like Max Moves.

G-Max Finale is a G-Max Move from Pokemon Sword and Shield , exclusive to Alcremie . Fairy type moves turn into G-Max Finale during Gigantamax. G-Max Finale deals damage and heals Pokémon on the user's side of the field.

G-Max Fireball is a G-Max Move from the Pokemon Sword and Shield Expansion Pass, exclusive to Cinderace . Fire type moves turn into G-Max Fireball during Gigantamax. G-Max Fireball deals damage and ignores all effects of the target's Ability. Its base power is always 160 , regardless of the original move selected, but it matches the original category (Physical or Special), like Max Moves.

G-Max Foam Burst is a G-Max Move from Pokémon Sword and Shield , exclusive to Kingler . Water type moves turn into G-Max Foam Burst during Gigantamax. G-Max Foam Burst deals damage and lowers the Speed of all opponents by two stages.

G-Max Gold Rush is a G-Max Move from Pokemon Sword and Shield , exclusive to Kantonian Meowth . Normal type moves turn into G-Max Gold Rush during Gigantamax. G-Max Gold Rush deals damage and confuses all opponents. It also earns extra money after the battle.

G-Max Gravitas is a G-Max Move from Pokémon Sword and Shield , exclusive to Orbeetle . Psychic type moves turn into G-Max Gravitas during Gigantamax. G-Max Gravitas deals damage and summons intense gravity for 5 turns, like the move Gravity .

G-Max Hydrosnipe is a G-Max Move from the Pokemon Sword and Shield Expansion Pass, exclusive to Inteleon . Water type moves turn into G-Max Hydrosnipe during Gigantamax. G-Max Hydrosnipe deals damage and ignores all effects of the target's Ability. G-Max Replenish is a G-Max Move from Pokémon Sword and Shield , exclusive to Snorlax . Normal type moves turn into G-Max Replenish during Gigantamax. G-Max Replenish deals damage and restores berries that have been eaten, so they can be used again.

G-Max Resonance is a G-Max Move from Pokemon Sword and Shield , exclusive to Lapras . Ice type moves turn into G-Max Resonance during Gigantamax. G-Max Resonance deals damage and reduces the damage received for 5 turns.

G-Max Sweetness is a G-Max Move from Pokémon Sword and Shield , exclusive to Appletun . Grass type moves turn into G-Max Sweetness during Gigantamax. G-Max Sweetness deals damage and heals any status conditions of the user and its teammates.

Giga Impact deals damage, but the user must recharge on the next turn (bringing its effective power down to 75 per turn). If the user has the ability Truant , it will recharge in the same turn that it loafs around . Giga Impact is the Physical counterpart to Hyper Beam .
Cannot be used twice in a row.
Gigavolt Havoc is the Electric type Z-Move, introduced in Pokémon Sun/Moon.

Glaciate deals damage and lowers the target's Speed by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Attacks from opposing Pokémon during the next turn cannot miss and will inflict double damage.

Glare paralyzes the target, if it hits. Paralyzed Pokémon have a 25% chance of not being able to attack, and their Speed is decreased by 50% (75% in Generations 1-6 ). Electric type Pokémon, those with the ability Limber or those behind a Substitute cannot be paralyzed.

Grass Pledge deals damage at base power 80 . If a teammate uses Fire Pledge or Water Pledge in the same turn, the moves combine into one attack of base power 160 and added effects appear. The faster Pokémon moves first but waits for the other Pokémon to make their move . When the slower Pledge user moves, the combo attack hits the Pokémon the slower user was targeting. If Grass Pledge is used with Fire Pledge , the move animation shows Fire Pledge and a sea of fire envelops the foe's team is created that damages opponents 1 ⁄ 8 of their maximum HP each turn, for 4 turns. If Grass Pledge is used with Water Pledge , the move animation shows Grass Pledge and a swamp envelops the foe's team that halves the opponents' speed for 4 turns.

Grass Whistle puts the target to sleep, if it hits. Sleeping Pokémon cannot move (with some exceptions such as Snore ). Sleep lasts for 1-3 turns. Pokémon with the abilities Insomnia or Vital Spirit , or those behind a Substitute cannot be put to sleep.
High priority during Grassy Terrain. Changes In Generation 8 , Grassy Glide has 70 base power.

Grassy Terrain creates a low-lying grassy field lasting 5 turns, that restores the HP of grounded Pokémon each turn, by 1 ⁄ 16 of their maximum HP. The power of Grass type moves also increases by 50% while in effect, and the moves Earthquake , Magnitude , and Bulldoze halve in power. Grounded Pokémon are simply those that are not raised . Raised Pokémon are any Flying types or those under the effects of Levitate , Magnet Rise or Telekinesis . Full details in glossary . Grassy Terrain is a terrain condition and can coexist at the same time as weather conditions, but not other terrain conditions. The move Nature Power turns into Energy Ball under Grassy Terrain .

Guard Split averages the user's Defense and Special Defense with those of the target. Stat boosts are not taken into account. For example, if the user's Defense was 100, the opponent's was 200, and the opponent had used Screech on the user, then both Pokémon would end up with 150 Defense. Due to the stat drop, the Guard Split user's effective Defense would now be 75 (instead of the 50 it was originally).

User and opponent swap Defense and Special Defense. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Psychium Z and uses its Z-Power, Guard Swap turns into Z-Guard Swap and raises Speed one stage, in addition to its usual effect as above.

If it hits, Guillotine is guaranteed to make the opponent faint. Its accuracy increases if the user is a higher level than the target Pokémon, but fails if the target is higher level. Gyro Ball inflicts varying damage: the slower the user compared to the opponent, the higher the damage, up to a maximum base power of 150 . The base power is calculated as: For example, if the opponent is twice as fast as the user, Gyro Ball will have base power 50.

The move Blizzard ignores accuracy. In Diamond/Pearl only, Blizzard has a 30% chance to break through Protect and Detect . The move Aurora Veil may be used. The power of Solar Beam and Solar Blade decreases by 50%. The moves Synthesis , Morning Sun and Moonlight recover 1 ⁄ 4 of the user's max HP (instead of the usual 1 ⁄ 2 HP). The move Weather Ball changes to Ice type and doubles in power. Pokémon with the ability Ice Body recover 1 ⁄ 16 of their max HP each turn. Pokémon with the ability Slush Rush double their speed. Pokémon with the ability Snow Cloak increase their evasion by 20%. Castform changes to Snowy Form.

Hidden Power deals damage, however its type varies for every Pokémon, depending on that Pokémon's Individual Values (IVs). This makes it a useful move for coverage - giving the Pokémon a type of move they don't normally have. Hidden Power can be any type except Normal or Fairy. It receives STAB if the user's type matches the type given to Hidden Power . Individual Values are 6 hidden numbers (one for each stat) that are randomly generated upon encountering a Pokémon. It is not possible to find your exact IVs in-game. You can find the type of Hidden Power in-game by talking to a particular character who will state the type a Pokémon's Hidden Power would be if it learned the move. They are located at: Veilstone Game Corner (Prize Exchange) in Platinum Celadon Game Corner (Prize Exchange) in HeartGold/SoulSilver Mistralton City (PokéCenter) in Black/White Pokémon World Tournament in Black 2/White 2 Anistar City (southeasternmost house) in X/Y. Fortree City (northwest) in Omega Ruby/Alpha Sapphire. Paniola Ranch (in the Pokémon Nursery) in Sun/Moon. It is also possible to determine via elimination by using the move against different Pokémon, or against Kecleon , whose ability Color Change will tell you the type.

Ice Ball deals damage for 5 turns, doubling in power each turn. The move stops if it misses on any turn. If it doesn't miss, Ice Ball will deal 30, 60, 120, 240 and 480 base power damage each turn respectively. If the user has used Defense Curl in any previous turn (without switching out) then Ice Ball doubles in power, i.e. it will deal 60, 120, 240, 480 and 960 base power damage respectively. As Ice Ball has 90% accuracy, the chances of it being successful for each turn (with normal Accuracy/Evasion and no other interruptions) are 90%, 81%, 73%, 66% and 59% respectively. In other words, roughly 3 out of every 5 times Ice Ball is used, it will hit for the full 5 turns.

If the Sturdy ability, Focus Sash , or Focus Band activates before the final hit, the subsequent hit will still deal damage and therefore cause the opponent to faint. If the first hit breaks a Substitute , the second hit will deal damage to the target Pokémon. The move Counter only counters the final hit, but Bide counters the complete damage. The Skill Link ability will cause Icicle Spear to always hit the maximum number of times.
Icy Wind deals damage and lowers the target's Speed by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.

Opponent is unable to use moves that the user also knows. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Psychium Z and uses its Z-Power, Imprison turns into Z-Imprison and raises Special Defense two stages, in addition to its usual effect as above.

Incinerate deals damage and if the target is holding a Berry, it is burnt and becomes no longer usable. If Incinerate would be super-effective and the target is holding an Occa Berry , the Berry activates before the move hits so does not get burnt.

Infernal Parade deals damage and has a 30% chance of burning the target. If the target already has a status condition, its power is doubled. Fire type Pokémon, those with the ability Water Veil or those behind a Substitute cannot be burned.

Inferno deals damage and burns the target, if it hits. Burned Pokémon lose 1 ⁄ 8 of their maximum HP each turn and their Attack is decreased by 50%. Fire type Pokémon, those with the ability Water Veil or those behind a Substitute cannot be burned.
Inferno Overdrive is the Fire type Z-Move, introduced in Pokémon Sun/Moon.

Infestation inflicts damage on the first turn then traps the opponent, causing them to lose 1 ⁄ 8 of their maximum HP after each turn, for 4-5 turns. If the user holds a Grip Claw , the after-effects last for 7 turns. If a Binding Band is held, the damage is 1 ⁄ 6 of the target's maximum HP.

The user of Ingrain plants it roots, which restores 1 ⁄ 16 of its maximum HP at the end of each turn, but prevents switching out. Only the moves Baton Pass , U Turn or Volt Switch will allow the user to switch out, with Baton Pass transferring the effects of Ingrain . Moves such as Roar used on this Pokémon will have no effect. Ingrain cancels effects given to raised Pokémon such as immunity to Ground -type moves, Spikes and so on. See more in the glossary . If the user is holding a Big Root , Ingrain restores 30% more HP each turn - approximately 1 ⁄ 12 of its maximum.

Instruct tells the user's teammate in a double/triple battle to use its most recent move instead of them. In other words, it allows a teammate to attack twice instead of the two Pokémon attacking once each.
Ion Deluge changes Normal-type moves to Electric-type for all Pokémon on the field, for the remainder of the turn.
Iron Defense raises the user's Defense by two stages. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.

Iron Head deals damage and has a 30% chance of causing the target to flinch (if the target has not yet moved). Pokémon with the ability Inner Focus or those behind a Substitute cannot be made to flinch.
Iron Tail deals damage and has a 30% chance of lowering the target's Defense by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.

Ivy Cudgel deals damage and has an increased critical hit ratio ( 1 ⁄ 8 instead of 1 ⁄ 24 ). Its type changes based on the mask worn by Ogerpon : Teal Mask form: Grass Wellspring Mask form: Water Heartflame Mask form: Fire Cornerstone Mask form: Rock
Prevents user and opponent from switching out.
Always goes first.
Type depends on the Arceus Plate being held. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Normalium Z and uses its Z-Power, Judgment turns into Breakneck Blitz and has base power 180 .
Jump Kick deals damage, however, if it misses the user keeps going and crashes, losing 1 ⁄ 2 of its maximum HP. Jump Kick cannot be used if Gravity is in effect.
Restores team's HP and cures status conditions.
Karate Chop deals damage and has an increased critical hit ratio ( 1 ⁄ 8 instead of 1 ⁄ 24 ).

Kinesis lowers the target's Accuracy by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each. Pokémon with the abilities Keen Eye , Clear Body or White Smoke cannot have their accuracy reduced.

King's Shield prevents any Physical or Special attacks targeted at the user from striking, for the duration of the turn. If a contact move is targeted at the King's Shield user, the opponent's Attack is lowered by two stages. It has priority +4 so will activate before most other moves. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each. The accuracy of King's Shield drops by 1 ⁄ 3 each time it is used consecutively after itself or Protect , Detect , Endure , Wide Guard , Quick Guard , Spiky Shield , or Baneful Bunker .

Knock Off deals damage and if the target has a held item, damage is increased by 50% (i.e. 97.5 base power) and the item is removed for the remainder of the battle (switching out does not restore the item). All items are restored at the end of a battle. If the item would activate on attack (e.g. Focus Band ), it does so before being stolen. If the item is consumable on attack (e.g. Focus Sash ) then the item is consumed as normal and cannot be stolen. Pokemon with the ability Sticky Hold cannot have their held item removed. The Griseous Orb cannot be removed from Giratina ; Type Plates cannot be removed from Arceus ; Drives cannot be removed from Genesect ; and Mega Stones cannot be removed from their respective Pokémon. Note that in these cases the damage dealt does not increase.
Always hits.
Land's Wrath deals damage with no additional effects.

User's next attack is guaranteed to result in a critical hit. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Normalium Z and uses its Z-Power, Laser Focus turns into Z-Laser Focus and raises Attack one stage, in addition to its usual effect as above.

The user lashes out to vent its frustration toward the target. If its stats were lowered during the turn, the power of this move is doubled. Lash Out is a tutor move available in The Isle of Armor DLC.

Last Resort deals high damage, but will fail if the user has not used each of its other moves at least once (or has no other moves). If the user switches out, the move gets reset and when it switches back in, will need to use each of its other moves at least once in order to use Last Resort again.
Damages increases the more party Pokémon have been defeated.
Lava Plume deals damage and has a 30% chance of burning the target. Fire type Pokémon, those with the ability Water Veil or those behind a Substitute cannot be burned.
Leaf Blade deals damage and has an increased critical hit ratio ( 1 ⁄ 8 instead of 1 ⁄ 24 ).
Leaf Storm deals damage but lowers the user's Special Attack by two stages after attacking. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.

Leaf Tornado deals damage and has a 30% chance of lowering the target's Accuracy by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each. Pokémon with the abilities Keen Eye , Clear Body or White Smoke cannot have their accuracy reduced.
Strikes opponent with leaves. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Grassium Z and uses its Z-Power, Leafage turns into Bloom Doom and has base power 100 .

Leech Life deals damage and the user will recover 50% of the HP drained. If the user is holding a Big Root , the move instead recovers 65% of the damage dealt (30% more than normal). If used on a Pokémon with the ability Liquid Ooze , the user instead loses the HP it would have otherwise gained.

Leech Seed plants a seed on the target that drains 1 ⁄ 8 of its maximum HP at the end of each turn and restores it to the user, or any Pokémon that takes its place. It does not work on Grass -type Pokémon; it does technically work against Pokémon with the Magic Guard ability, but no HP will be sapped. If the user is holding a Big Root , the move still saps the same amount of HP but recovers 30% more than normal. For example, if a Pokémon has 320 HP, Leech Seed would cause them to lose 40 HP each turn but the seeder would recover 52 HP. If used on a Pokémon with the ability Liquid Ooze , the user instead loses the HP it would have otherwise gained. The seeded Pokémon can clear Leech Seed by switching out or using Rapid Spin .
Leer lowers the target's Defense by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Let's Snuggle Forever is a Fairy type Z-Move exclusive to Mimikyu . It is used with Play Rough .
Lick deals damage and has a 30% chance of paralyzing the target. Electric type Pokémon, those with the ability Limber or those behind a Substitute cannot be paralyzed.
Life Dew recovers up to 25% of the user's and its teammate's maximum HP.
Light of Ruin deals damage, but the user receives 1 ⁄ 2 of the damage it inflicted in recoil. In other words, if the attack does 100 HP damage to the opponent, the user will lose 50 HP.

Light Screen reduces damage from Special attacks by 50%, for 5 turns. Its effects apply to all Pokémon on the user's side of the field. If the user is holding Light Clay when Light Screen is used, its effects last for 8 turns. The move Brick Break , when used against an Pokémon under Light Screen's effect, will remove the screen before dealing damage. The ability Infiltrator bypasses the effects of Light Screen . Light Screen is the Special counterpart to Reflect .

Light That Burns the Sky is a Psychic type Z-Move exclusive to Necrozma . It is used with Photon Geyser . Light That Burns the Sky ignores target's ability and inflicts damage based on the user's Attack or Special Attack, whichever is higher.

Mirror Shot deals damage and has a 30% chance of lowering the target's Accuracy by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each. Pokémon with the abilities Keen Eye , Clear Body or White Smoke cannot have their accuracy reduced.

User's stats cannot be changed for a period of time. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Icium Z and uses its Z-Power, Mist turns into Z-Mist and fully restores the user's HP, in addition to its usual effect as above.
Mist Ball deals damage and has a 50% chance of lowering the target's Special Attack by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
The user attacks everything around it and faints upon using this move. This move’s power is increased on Misty Terrain. Misty Explosion is a tutor move available in The Isle of Armor DLC.

Misty Terrain creates a low-lying mist lasting 5 turns, that protects all grounded Pokémon from status conditions (including confusion). It also halves the power of Dragon type moves while in effect. Grounded Pokémon are simply those that are not raised . Raised Pokémon are any Flying types or those under the effects of Levitate , Magnet Rise or Telekinesis . Full details in glossary . Misty Terrain is a terrain condition and can coexist at the same time as weather conditions, but not other terrain conditions. The move Nature Power turns into Moonblast under Misty Terrain .
Moonblast deals damage and has a 30% chance of lowering the target's Special Attack by one stage.

Ignores the target's ability. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Ghostium Z and uses its Z-Power, Moongeist Beam turns into Never-Ending Nightmare and has base power 180 . When Lunala or Dawn Wings Necrozma is holding Lunalium Z and uses its Z-Power, Moongeist Beam turns into Menacing Moonraze Maelstrom and has base power 200 .
The user restores its own HP. The amount of HP regained varies with the weather.

User recovers HP. Amount varies with the weather. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Normalium Z and uses its Z-Power, Morning Sun turns into Z-Morning Sun and eliminates any stat drops applied to the user, in addition to its usual effect as above.
Removes entry hazards and trap move effects, and poisons opposing Pokémon.

Mountain Gale deals damage and has a 30% chance of causing the target to flinch (if the target has not yet moved). Pokémon with the ability Inner Focus or those behind a Substitute cannot be made to flinch.

Mud Shot deals damage and lowers the target's Speed by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Mud Sport reduces the power of Electric type moves by 50%. The effect applies to the entire field. Most Pokémon that learn Mud Sport are part Ground type and thus already immune to Electric attacks.

Mud-Slap deals damage and lowers the target's Accuracy by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each. Pokémon with the abilities Keen Eye , Clear Body or White Smoke cannot have their accuracy reduced.

Muddy Water deals damage and has a 30% chance of lowering the target's Accuracy by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each. Pokémon with the abilities Keen Eye , Clear Body or White Smoke cannot have their accuracy reduced.

Nasty Plot raises the user's Special Attack by two stages. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.

Halves the foe's HP. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Fairium Z and uses its Z-Power, Nature's Madness turns into Twinkle Tackle and has base power 100 . When a Tapu is holding Tapunium Z and uses its Z-Power, Nature's Madness turns into Guardian of Alola .

Night Daze deals damage and has a 40% chance of lowering the target's Accuracy by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each. Pokémon with the abilities Keen Eye , Clear Body or White Smoke cannot have their accuracy reduced.
The damage of Night Shade is equal to the user's level. So at level 100 the Pokémon will inflict 100 HP damage.
Night Slash deals damage and has an increased critical hit ratio ( 1 ⁄ 8 instead of 1 ⁄ 24 ).
If the target is asleep, Nightmare causes it to lose 1 ⁄ 4 of its maximum HP after each turn. The status condition is cured when the Pokémon wakes up.

Nuzzle deals damage and paralyzes the target. Paralyzed Pokémon have a 25% chance of not being able to attack, and their Speed is decreased by 50% (75% in Generations 1-6 ). Electric type Pokémon, those with the ability Limber or those behind a Substitute cannot be paralyzed.

Oblivion Wing deals damage and the user will recover 75% of the HP drained. Oblivion Wing is the signature move of Yveltal . In battle, the user flies high into the sky then releases a beam of red light that scorches the ground.

Obstruct prevents any attacks targeted at the user from striking, for the duration of the turn. If a contact move is targeted at the user on that turn, the attacker's Defense is lowered by two stages. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Oceanic Operetta is a Water type Z-move exclusive to Primarina . It is used with Primarina's signature move Sparkling Aria .

Octazooka deals damage and has a 50% chance of lowering the target's Accuracy by one stage. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each. Pokémon with the abilities Keen Eye , Clear Body or White Smoke cannot have their accuracy reduced.

Octolock prevents the target from fleeing or switching out, while the user remains in battle. It also lowers the target's Defense and Special Defense by one stage each every turn. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.

Resets opponent's Evasiveness, and allows Normal- and Fighting-type attacks to hit Ghosts. Changes In Generation 3 , Odor Sleuth has 100% accuracy. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Normalium Z and uses its Z-Power, Odor Sleuth turns into Z-Odor Sleuth and raises Attack one stage, in addition to its usual effect as above.
Ominous Wind deals damage and has a 10% chance of raising the user's Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense and Speed by one stage each. Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.

Order Up deals damage. If the user, Dondozo, has a Tatsugiri in its mouth, it also raises a particular stat depending on the form of Tatsugiri: Curly Form (orange) raises Dondozo's Attack Droopy Form (pink) raises Dondozo's Defense Stretchy Form (yellow) raises Dondozo's Speed Stats can be raised to a maximum of +6 stages each.
Origin Pulse deals damage and hits all adjacent opponents in double/triple battles. It is the signature move of Kyogre .

The user of Outrage attacks for 2-3 turns, during which it cannot switch out, and then becomes confused. Confused Pokémon have a 33% chance of hurting themselves each turn, for 1-4 attacking turns (50% chance in Generations 1-6 ). The damage received is as if the Pokémon attacks itself with a typeless 40 base power Physical attack. If Outrage is disrupted (e.g. if the move misses or the user cannot attack due to paralysis) then it will stop and not cause confusion. Pokémon with the ability Own Tempo or those behind a Substitute cannot be confused.
Hits all adjacent opponents.
Overheat deals damage but lowers the user's Special Attack by two stages after attacking. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.

The user's and opponent's HP becomes the average of both. Changes In Generation 2 , Pain Split has 100% accuracy. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Normalium Z and uses its Z-Power, Pain Split turns into Z-Pain Split and raises Defense one stage, in addition to its usual effect as above.
Parabolic Charge deals damage and the user will recover 50% of the HP drained. It targets all adjacent Pokémon.
Parting Shot lowers the opponent's Attack and Special Attack by one stage each, then the user switches out of battle. Stats can be lowered to a minimum of -6 stages each.
Money is earned after the battle. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Normalium Z and uses its Z-Power, Pay Day turns into Breakneck Blitz and has base power 100 .
Power doubles if the user was attacked first. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Darkinium Z and uses its Z-Power, Payback turns into Black Hole Eclipse and has base power 100 .
Peck deals damage with no additional effect, besides being able to hit non-adjacent Pokémon in triple battles.

Any Pokémon in play when this attack is used faints in 3 turns. Z-Move effects When a Pokémon is holding Normalium Z and uses its Z-Power, Perish Song turns into Z-Perish Song and eliminates any stat drops applied to the user, in addition to its usual effect as above.
Petal Blizzard deals damage with no additional effect. In double battles it will hit all Pokémon on the field; in triple battles it will hit all adjacent Pokémon.

The user of Petal Dance attacks for 2-3 turns, during which it cannot switch out, and then becomes confused. If Petal Dance is disrupted (e.g. if the move misses or the user cannot attack due to paralysis) then it will stop and not cause confusion. Pokémon with the ability Own Tempo or those behind a Substitute cannot be confused.
Phantom Force causes the user to disappear on the first turn, becoming invulnerable to most attacks. On the second turn, it deals damage and can hit Pokémon using Protect or Detect .
Photon Geyser deals damage, but uses either the user's Attack or Special Attack stat to determine its power, whichever is higher.

Pika Papow is a Pikachu-exclusive Partner Power from Pokémon Let's Go. The power increases when Pikachu's Friendship is higher (similar to Return ), up to a maximum base power of 102 . When a different Pokémon is in battle, Pika Papow may be used to boost that Pokémon's stats.

Hits Prob. Power 2 3 ⁄ 8 50 3 3 ⁄ 8 75 4 1 ⁄ 8 100 5 1 ⁄ 8 125 Pin Missile hits 2-5 times per turn used. The probability of each interval is shown in the table, with the total power after each hit. Each strike of Pin Missile is treated like a separate attack: Each strike can get a critical hit independently. If the Sturdy ability, Focus Sash , or Focus Band activates before the final hit, the subsequent hit will still deal damage and therefore cause the opponent to faint.
